title: Ghost White-Label
html:
Ghost White-Label

Rankett's specific architectural commitment: zero Rankett branding anywhere — not in UI, page source, meta tags, error messages, login flows, admin screens, or fulfillment communications. Agency's own domain (e.g., visibility.agencyname.com), agency's logo, colors, favicon, company name. Stronger than vanilla white-label, which often allows some "powered by X" footer or admin-screen leak. Ghost white-label is the moat-shaped version: clients can't bypass to "go direct" because they don't know Rankett exists.

The architecture appears in fully-formed shape in 2026 01 07 rankett meetup prep guide (the 25KB GTM bible) — "Ghost white-label — zero Rankett branding anywhere (UI, page source, meta tags, error messages). Agency's domain, logo, colors. Clients never know Rankett exists." The strategic point is non-cosmetic: ghost-mode commits Rankett to never building a direct-to-end-client business, which is itself the nonobvious move that makes the agency channel defensible. By eliminating its own brand, Rankett eliminates channel conflict — agencies don't have to fear that Rankett will eventually compete with them. The brand-absence is the trust primitive.

The supporting architecture is documented across four other notes. 2025 08 29 irl lore prd — the IRL Lore PRD — references ghost-white-label tooling as a "same tone-as-product instinct" for that earlier app, suggesting the brand-invisibility pattern is one Yahir tried elsewhere first. The 2025 12 19 rankett 12 month business plan formalizes ghost-white-label as one of the 9 hard non-negotiables (alongside "no C-tier clients", "no custom builds for one partner", "no channel conflict"). The 2026 01 02 diddles call notes scope it precisely: "TOOL whitelabeled (login + branding + onboarding portal + stats + client updates) but NOT the technical implementations themselves" — meaning the agency-facing surface is invisible, while the technical work being shipped to client sites (schema markup, llm.txt, directory updates) is visible by necessity. The April 2026 build doc (2026 04 27 rankett com build doc) operationalizes ghost-white-label as the foundation of the partner program: "the invisible technical partner for marketing agencies… you do it. Ghost white-labeled. The client never knows you exist."

The deeper bet under ghost-white-label is structural. Vanilla white-label is a delivery choice; ghost is a category choice. By committing to invisibility, Rankett positions itself as infrastructure rather than as a competitor product. AWS for AI search visibility, not Squarespace. The pricing model (tier ladder: Lambo / Fred Meyer / Walmart) and the 60 day activation rule both rest on this — agencies are buying capacity, not a brand. The economic argument from 2026 01 02 diddles call makes it explicit: "would you rather net $2500 from each client and not do a bit of work or do $3500 and do an extra 15 hours of work, and build the tools and systems" — agencies pay for the invisible work because the invisibility is the value.

  • 2025 12 19 rankett 12 month business plan
  • 2026 01 02 diddles call
  • 2026 01 07 rankett meetup prep guide
  • 2026 04 27 rankett com build doc
